title: eriogonum vimineum subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum vimineum subsp. typicum, also known as the Nevada State flower, is a perennial wildflower native to the Great Basin region in North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Eriogonum vimineum subsp. typicum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
